description: Implement reliable DS-160 CEAC autofill to the Sign and Submit page without final submission.

userStories.title: Pin CEAC automation scope and handoff contract
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the CEAC automation scope codified in the repo so the worker targets the Sign and Submit page and stops there.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/prd-ds160-ceac-autofill.md exists in the repo
prd.json contains DS-160 CEAC autofill stories instead of unrelated chatbot stories
The repo branch for this run is ralph/ds160-ceac-clean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep this story docs-only and use it to unblock the rest of the CEAC automation run.

userStories.title: Add CEAC session bootstrap and page identity helpers
userStories.description: As a worker, I need stable CEAC session and page-detection helpers so I can start and route the DS-160 flow reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: submission-service has CEAC helpers for session start, page identity detection, and common selectors
Page identity checks are explicit and not based only on timing
Unexpected page states raise structured errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scope changes to viza-be/submission-service CEAC modules only.

userStories.title: Normalize page-by-page CEAC navigation
userStories.description: As a worker, I need deterministic navigation across DS-160 pages so the flow can progress section by section without silent failures.
acceptanceCriteria: CEAC navigation is routed through a central page router or equivalent abstraction
Next-page transitions verify destination page identity
Validation failures are detected and surfaced with page context
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not add submission behavior; only improve navigation and validation.

userStories.title: Capture Application ID and save checkpoints
userStories.description: As ops, I need Application ID capture and save checkpoints so a partially completed DS-160 run remains recoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: The worker captures DS-160 Application ID as early as it becomes available
The worker performs save/checkpoint actions at natural section boundaries
Checkpoint metadata is persisted or logged in structured form
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Prefer minimally invasive changes to existing submission-service persistence points.

userStories.title: Support .dat artifact capture and recovery metadata
userStories.description: As ops, I need `.dat` artifact handling and recovery metadata so failed or paused runs can be resumed.
acceptanceCriteria: When CEAC offers Save to File, the worker captures the `.dat` artifact path or file output
Recovery metadata includes Application ID and last successful checkpoint
Failure paths preserve recovery information when available
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: If full upload/replay is not yet wired, at minimum capture and structure the artifacts and metadata.

userStories.title: Implement stop-at-sign-page contract
userStories.description: As VIZA, I need the worker to reach the final Sign and Submit page and stop before irreversible actions.
acceptanceCriteria: The worker detects the final Sign and Submit page using explicit markers
The worker does not enter passport-number signature
The worker does not enter CAPTCHA or click final Sign and Submit
The stop state is logged as a successful handoff-ready outcome
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core product boundary; do not blur it with auto-submit behavior.

userStories.title: Add success and failure artifacts for CEAC runs
userStories.description: As ops, I need screenshots and structured diagnostics so I can verify sign-page reach and debug failures quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Successful runs store a sign-page screenshot or equivalent artifact reference
Failure runs store error context with last page/checkpoint
Artifact paths or references are emitted in worker output/logging
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Treat the sign-page screenshot as proof of success.

userStories.title: Return handoff-ready result payload
userStories.description: As internal ops, I need a handoff-ready result payload so I can give the client their Application ID and retrieval instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker success output includes Application ID, stop-state, and available recovery artifacts
Output clearly distinguishes handoff-ready from submitted
No code path performs final DS-160 submission
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep output contract focused on internal ops handoff, not user-facing UI work.
